gpt_critique.critique: Model B provides a more comprehensive analysis, particularly in Decent Work and Economic Growth, Responsible Consumption and Production, Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions, and Partnerships for the Goals.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Model B's focus on technological upgrading aligns better with course content.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Model B highlights relevant course topics on resource efficiency and sustainability.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Model B's emphasis on XBRL's role in transparency aligns with course objectives.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Model B's focus on data analysis tools supports the course's emphasis on capacity building.

gemini_critique.critique: Model A provided a more comprehensive analysis, identifying several SDGs I initially missed or underestimated. Its reasoning for technological upgrading, transparency, and sustainability reporting is well-supported by the course text.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Model A better links new technologies (AI, RPA, blockchain) and system integration to technological upgrading and higher economic productivity (Target 8.2).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Model A correctly identifies production cycle management (PP module) for efficiency and XBRL for integrating sustainability reporting (Target 12.6).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Model A's reasoning that XBRL for transparent financial reporting contributes to effective, accountable institutions (Target 16.6) is valid.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Model A correctly links data analysis tools and XBRL standards to supporting reliable data (Target 17.18) and knowledge sharing (Target 17.6).
